title: Red Wine Reduction Sauce
description: A classic pan sauce with shallot, garlic, thyme, and butter. Ready in 20 minutes. Works over steak, pork chops, or roasted chicken.

ingredients.name: salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions.text: Melt 1 tbsp butter in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add shallot and garlic and sauté until softened and fragrant, about 2–3 minutes.
Pour in the red wine, stirring to scrape up any bits from the bottom of the skillet. Bring to a simmer.
Add stock and thyme. Simmer gently, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces by half, about 10–15 minutes.
Taste and adjust -- if too acidic, stir in the sugar. Season with salt and pepper.
Remove from heat and whisk in the remaining 1 tbsp butter for a glossy finish. Strain through a fine mesh sieve if you prefer a smoother texture. Serve warm.

notes:
The butter finish off heat is important -- adding butter while the pan is still on heat can break the sauce.

For a richer sauce, use beef stock and a full-bodied red like Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot.

For vegetarian, use vegetable stock and the sauce works well over roasted mushrooms or root vegetables.
